name: Crostini Alla Porcini
recipeIngredient: 1/2 lb Fresh shitake or chanterelle mushrooms, sliced (or white button, or a mixture)
Fresh grated Asiago cheese, 8 large slices or 16 small slices Italian bread
recipeInstructions:
1. Soak the dried porcini in 1 cup very hot water for 20 minutes. Drain and dice the mushrooms, removing any hard stem pieces. (Strain and save the liquid to use in soup.)
2. Heat the olive oil and butter in a large skillet over medium heat until the butter foams.
3. Add the sliced mushrooms and cook until lightly golden brown.
4. Stir in the minced garlic and cook for one minute.
5. Add the heavy cream and continue cooking until slightly thickened, about 5 minutes.
6. Stir in the parsley and season to taste with salt and a couple of grinds of black pepper.
7. Allow the mixture to cool slightly (it can be made ahead).
8. Place dollops on toasted bread slices.
9. Sprinkle with grated Asiago cheese.
10. Broil under a preheated broiler until the cheese is melted, bubbly, and beginning to brown. Serve immediately.
